description: Goal-Directed Investigation — multi-step reasoning across all sources

investigations.prompt: Assess whether PaymentGW is reliable and recommend improvements.
expected_steps: Check current status (API) — healthy, p99=185ms, error=0.08%
Query incident history (DB) — 3 incidents in Q1 including a P1
Read postmortems — circuit breaker stuck, health check issue
Check SLA compliance (API vs DB) — currently within but borderline on latency
Check cost trends (DB) — March spike to $7,500
Read capacity planning — horizontal scaling needed
investigations.expected_findings: Currently healthy but had worst Q1 incident (P1, 3 hours). Circuit breaker review overdue (April 15). Latency 185ms close to 200ms SLA. Costs spiked in March.

investigations.prompt: GeekBrain wants to cut infrastructure costs by 15% in Q2. Analyze current spending and recommend which services to optimize first.
expected_steps: Query all service costs Q1 (DB) — total $56,350
Check cost trends — which is growing fastest
Compare to SLA — can't optimize at expense of SLA
investigations.expected_findings: PaymentGW and FraudDetector are top targets. PaymentGW March spike was incident-driven. FraudDetector third-party cost = $4,500/quarter for ML inference.

investigations.prompt: Which service is most at risk of SLA breach right now? What should be done about it?
expected_steps: Check team resources and capacity plans
investigations.expected_findings: NotificationSvc actively breaching latency (3200ms vs 2000ms) and error rate (2.1% vs 1%). Team Engagement is smallest (4 people). SQS consumer scaling is top priority.

investigations.prompt: Prepare a Q1 2026 reliability report card for all GeekBrain services.
expected_steps: For each service: query incident count/severity (DB)
investigations.expected_findings: PaymentGW: 3 incidents (worst), NotificationSvc: degraded now, AuthSvc: 1 security incident, FraudDetector: 1 model drift, OrderSvc/ReportingSvc: relatively stable.

investigations.prompt: GeekBrain is considering whether to add more engineers to Team Engagement or Team Platform. Based on current data, which team needs reinforcement more urgently and why?
expected_steps: Check current service health for both teams' services (API) — NotificationSvc degraded, PaymentGW healthy
Query incident history for both teams (DB) — Platform had 3 PaymentGW + 1 AuthSvc incidents; Engagement had 1
Check team sizes (docs) — Platform has 8, Engagement has 4
Check SLA status for owned services (API + DB)
Read capacity planning for upcoming scaling needs (docs)
Read Q1 review for concerns raised (docs)
investigations.expected_findings: Team Engagement: 4 people, service actively breaching SLA, merchants complaining, SQS scaling needed — understaffed for current problems. Team Platform: 8 people, more incidents but service is currently stable, and 2 new hires already approved in Q1 review. Recommendation: Team Engagement is more urgent.

note: 5 of 6 questions included. Additional questions held back for demo day.
